title: Medical device
abstract: A medical device (10) includes a flexible tube (12), wherein a portion (20) of the flexible tube (12) is in the shape of a helix that is extendable from: (i) a neutral condition, in which the tube (12) is not subject to tension, to (ii) an extended condition under the influence of a tensile force. The axial length of the helical portion (20) of the tube (12) in the extended condition is at least 1.2 times the axial length of the helical portion (20) of the tube (12) in the neutral condition. The medical device (10) further includes a sleeve (14) that extends around the helical portion (20) of the tube (12).
claims:
Claims 1. A medical device including: a flexible tube, wherein a portion of the tube is in the shape of a helix that is extendable from: a neutral condition, in which the tube is not subject to tension, to an extended condition under the influence of a tensile force, wherein the axial length of the helical portion of the tube in the extended condition is at least 1.2 times the axial length of the helical portion of the tube in the neutral condition; and a sleeve that extends around the helical portion of the tube. 2. The medical device according to claim 1 , wherein a first axial end of the sleeve is secured to the tube at or near a first axial end of the helical portion of the tube. 3. The medical device according to claim 2, wherein the tube and sleeve are not secured to each other otherwise than at the first axial end of the sleeve, such that extension of the helical portion of the tube from the neutral condition to the extended condition causes relative axial movement of the tube within the sleeve otherwise than at the first axial end of the sleeve. 4. The medical device according to claim 3, wherein the inner diameter of the sleeve is at least 1 mm greater than the outer diameter of the helical portion of the tube in the neutral condition. 5. The medical device according to claim 4, wherein the axial length of the sleeve is greater than the axial length of the helical portion of the tube when the tube is in the extended condition. 6. The medical device according to claim 5, wherein the axial length of the sleeve is between 1.2 and 1.5 times the axial length of the helical portion of the tube when in the neutral condition. 7. The medical device according to claim 1 , wherein the sleeve is made of an elastomeric material. 8. The medical device according to claim 7, wherein the helical portion of the tube is snugly received within the sleeve, inducing hoop stresses in the sleeve. 9. The medical device according to claim 8, wherein the sleeve is arranged around the helical portion of the tube such that extension of the helical portion of the tube causes corresponding axial extension of the sleeve. 10. The medical device according to claim 9, wherein the sleeve is arranged around the helical portion of the tube such that extension of the helical portion of the tube from the neutral condition to the extended condition does not cause relative movement between the helical portion of the tube on the one hand and the sleeve on the other hand. 1 1. The medical device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sleeve is made of a transparent material. 12. The medical device according to claim 2, further including an extensible guide that includes: a body in the shape of a helix with a substantially constant radius of curvature along the length of the body, the body being axially resiliently deformable between: (iv) a neutral condition, in which the body is not subject to axial compressive or tensile forces; (v) an extended condition, in which the body is subject to an axial tensile force; and (vi) a retracted condition, in which the body is subject to an axial compressive force; and 

 means for securing the tube to the body, thereby to cause the tube to assume the helical shape of the body, characterised in that when the body is in the neutral condition, the extended condition or the retracted condition, the radius of curvature of the body remains substantialiy constant along the length of the body. 13. The medical device according to claim 12, wherein the body is sufficiently elastically deformable such that, when the body is subject to an axial tensile force of at least 0.2N, the axial length of the body in the extended condition is at least 1.2 times the axial length of the body in the neutral condition. 14. The medical device according to claim 13, wherein the securing means comprises at least one resilient formation defined by the body, in use to: deform; permit the tube to pass by the resilient formation; and clip the tube to the body. 15. The medical device according to claim 14, wherein the body is generally U-shaped in axial cross-section, defining: a base; and a pair of arms extending laterally from axial ends of the base. 16. The medical device according to claim 15, wherein the arms converge towards their free ends, thereby providing the resilient formation. 17. The medical device according to claim 16, wherein the body is shaped such that the tube is radially received by, and secured to the body along: the entire length of the body; or at at least two points per turn of the helical body. 18. The medical device according to claim 17, wherein the body is made of a transparent material. 19. The medical device according to claim 18, wherein the body does not extend circumferentially around the tube, thereby radially exposing a portion of the tube. 20. The medical device according to claim 19, wherein the tube is secured to the body: along the length of the body; or at at least two points per turn of the helical body.
